audit_items.proof_trace: sass_make_error has exactly two allocations: calloc for v (guarded by `if (v == 0) return 0`) and sass_copy_c_string for message (guarded by `if (v->error.message == 0) { free(v); return 0 }`). sass_alloc_memory (sass.cpp L37-43) calls exit() on OOM so sass_copy_c_string never returns NULL; the null-message guard only fires when msg==NULL, in which case v is correctly freed before returning. All error paths properly free allocated memory.
